tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, proteinuria, ketonuria, elevated bilirubin, and low specific gravity, indicating possible tubular dysfunction or acute kidney injury. Hemoglobin A1c is low, which may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, assessment for underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders, and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
